Elective Course
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UED2  |
VHS:
21h  |
Weekly hours:
1h30  |
Lecture:
1h30
Coefficient:
1  |
Credits:
1
Teaching Objectives
This course allows students to acquire a broader view of their field of study and promotes a better understanding of the social, economic, and ethical issues related to computer science, as well as the ability to communicate effectively and adapt to diverse situations. Specifically, this course enables:
An opening to other disciplines related to everyday life, in order to understand the importance of collaboration in a multidisciplinary environment
A broadening of horizons, favouring understanding of human thought across time and space
Development of transversal skills: communication, holistic vision, analysis and synthesis, adaptability, etc.
Course Content (21h)
Introduction to the relevant discipline (chosen subject)
Presentation of fundamental concepts
Cross-applications with computer science
Case studies
Suggested elective topics include:
Green Computing
Governance and Digital Transformation
Philosophy of Science and Technology
Digital Law and Data Protection (GDPR)
Cognitive Psychology
Emerging Technologies (Blockchain, IoT, ...)
Discovery of master specialisations
Assessment
Final examination (in person): 100%
Bibliography
References proposed by the instructor according to the chosen subject.
